title: Aspidium monticola
taxonomy: Aspidium monticola is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a part of the genus Aspidium, which is now often considered a synonym for the genus Dryopteris. Aspidium monticola's classification is further grouped under the family Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The species is native to certain regions of the world, but specific details about its native range are not readily available. Further research is necessary to confirm the exact classification and distribution of Aspidium monticola.

intro: Aspidium monticola, also known as Mountain maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium monticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain maidenhair fern include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and relieve symptoms of respiratory infections.
